focus_points.label: Late-night load — what’s driving it now
focus_points.reason: Notes say she shipped ahead after weeks of late nights and has been downplaying concern. Could be a temporary sprint crunch, a lingering habit, or extra hidden work now that pressure is over—worth clarifying what’s actually creating the current load.

focus_points.label: What she’s saying yes and no to
focus_points.reason: Whether her near-term priorities match the real post-sprint reality, or whether she’s still carrying urgency forward. This helps you separate output from sustainability and get her sequencing straight for the next cycle.

focus_points.label: Recognition and credit she’s not getting
focus_points.reason: Notes mention strong execution without recognition can quietly drain engagement. Could be that wins aren’t landing publicly, or that she wants different kinds of acknowledgment—worth clarifying what “seen” looks like for her right now.

focus_points.label: Visibility and influence bottlenecks
focus_points.reason: Where she feels blocked in visibility and influence is the likely driver of stalled decisions or rerouted work. Clarifying the specific dependency or information gap will tell you what to intervene on and what to unblock.

focus_points.label: What she wants more from you
focus_points.reason: Bi-weekly check-ins for a Senior IC work best when you explicitly ask what support she wants from you (air cover, decisions, introductions, or coaching) so she can keep momentum without silently absorbing friction.
